Home > PUBLICATIONS - Investigation Reports (Aircraft)

Aircraft Accident and Incident Reports

The objective of the investigation conducted by the Japan Transport Safety Board in accordance with the Act for Establishment of the Japan Transport Safety Board and with Annex 13 to the Convention on International Civil Aviation is to determine the causes of aircraft accidents and serious incidents and damage incidental to aircraft accidents, thereby preventing future accidents and incidents, and reducing damage. It is not the purpose of the investigation to apportion blame or liability.

The English versions of these reports are translations of the Japanese original investigation reports. The text in Japanese versions shall prevail in the interpretation of the reports.The English versions of these reports have been translated and issued by the JTSB for reference purposes only. Not all investigation reports published by the JTSB are available in English.

Year of Occurrence:

Reference

The terms used to describe the results of the analysis in "3. ANALYSIS" of this report are as follows.
i) In case of being able to determine, the term "certain" or "certainly" is used.
ii) In case of being unable to determine but being almost certain, the term "highly probable" or "most likely" is used.
iii) In case of higher possibility, the term "probable" or "more likely" is used.
iv) In a case that there is a possibility, the term "likely" or "possible" is used.

Top of this page
